title: Sev Tameta Nu Shaak

overview.description: A classic Gujarati tomato curry finished with crispy sev. Sweet, tangy and gently spiced, it's a quick meal that comes together in under 30 minutes.

method: Heat the oil over a medium heat.
Add the mustard seeds and allow them to pop.
Add the cumin seeds, curry leaves and asafoetida and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in the ginger and green chillies and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes and cook for 10–12 minutes until they break down into a thick sauce.
Stir in the turmeric, ground coriander, ground cumin and Kashmiri chilli powder.
Add the jaggery, amchur and salt.
Taste and adjust the balance of sweetness, acidity and seasoning.
Stir through half the sev just before serving.
Scatter the remaining sev and fresh coriander over the top immediately before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Sev Tameta is one of Gujarat's most recognisable home-style dishes and is traditionally made when fresh vegetables are limited.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance sweet, sour and savoury flavours, and how to preserve texture by adding sev at the last moment.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fast pantry meal with surprisingly complex flavours that uses ingredients already found in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Use ripe tomatoes for the best flavour.
Taste before serving—the sweetness and acidity should be in balance.
Only add the sev immediately before serving or it will lose its texture.
Roasted cumin powder makes an excellent finishing garnish alongside the coriander.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the tomato curry only. Add fresh sev after reheating.
notes.leftovers: The curry reheats well, but always add fresh sev just before serving.
